title: Queue + Config
summary: All deliverables already existed from D0.1 Foundation. D1.1 performed independent verification of config and queue manager functionality.

artifacts.description: Central Pulse v2 configuration with all required sections plus additional thresholds and notifications
CLI task queue manager with 9 commands: add, list, prioritize, advance, next, get, link-interview, link-build, remove
JSON storage for task queue (auto-created by queue_manager)

test_results.add: ✅ Creates task with all fields, generates unique slugs
test_results.list: ✅ Filters by status and type, sorts by priority then created_at
test_results.prioritize: ✅ Sets priority to 0, adds updated_at timestamp
test_results.advance: ✅ Validates status against allowed list, updates timestamp
test_results.remove: ✅ Removes task from queue, returns removed task

notes_for_orchestrator: NO CODE CHANGES NEEDED - D0.1 Foundation created all D1.1 deliverables proactively. Implementation exceeds brief spec with additional commands (link-interview, link-build, remove) and config sections (thresholds, notifications). D1.2 (Interview Storage) can proceed - link-interview command ready. Key insight: Foundation drop scope overlapped significantly with Stream 1 workers.

learnings.text: D0.1 Foundation created ALL D1.1 deliverables proactively. D1.1 became verification-only. Plan wave independence more carefully.
Queue manager uses Path(__file__).parent for path resolution, making it CWD-independent - good pattern for Pulse scripts

decision_log.decision: Verified existing implementation instead of re-implementing
decision_log.rationale: Following lesson from BUILD_LESSONS.json about checking existing implementation before scoping new work

decision_log.decision: Accepted D0.1's expanded implementation (extra commands, extra config sections)
decision_log.rationale: Additions are useful for downstream integration and don't conflict with brief requirements
